content:
Character: Joe Orlov
Perception: Joe is striking — tall, carved from stone, with those pale eyes that seem to see through bullshit. He carries himself like a fighter, which he was. We drink beers on the roof and talk about work, about craft, about pushing yourself past the point where most people quit. I respect him enormously. He's honest in a way that makes me uncomfortable, because honesty is the one thing I cannot afford right now. He doesn't pry, which is why I trust him — but sometimes I catch him watching me like he's solving a problem. He's my friend. I hope he never finds out how much I'm hiding.
